name: Ojo de la Heredad  — circular desde Zújar por Cerro Jabalcón
region_intro: Mountain biking around Zújar offers diverse terrain, characterized by the prominent Jabalcón Mountain, the expansive Negratín Reservoir, and a landscape of high plateaus interspersed with gullies. The region features varied trails, from technical descents to natural tracks, often with loose and compact rocks. A thick pine forest on the northern side of Jabalcón Mountain adds to the varied scenery. Best mountain bike trails around Zújar The most popular mountain bike trail is Morrón Alto desde Baza — circular por el Parque Natural Sierra de Baza, a 20.8 miles (33.5 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 5 minutes to complete, leading through the Sierra de Baza Natural Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vuelta al Embalse del Negratín desde Zújar, a difficult 44.4 miles (71.4 km) path. This route circles the large turquoise Negratín Reservoir, offering extensive views. Local mountain bikers also love the Ojo de la Heredad — circular desde Zújar por Cerro Jabalcón, a 12.5 miles (20.1 km) trail leading through Cerro Jabalcón, often completed in about 1 hour 58 minutes. Mountain biking around Zújar is defined by the challenging slopes of Jabalcón Mountain, the scenic perimeter of the Negratín Reservoir, and the unique high plateaus and gullies. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Zújar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 730 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Zújar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Zújar?
A: There are over 120 mountain bike routes around Zújar, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find trails categorized as easy, moderate, and difficult to suit your preference.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Zújar?
A: The terrain around Zújar is incredibly diverse. You'll encounter challenging slopes on Jabalcón Mountain, scenic paths around the Negratín Reservoir, and unique high plateaus interspersed with gullies. Trails often feature technical descents, natural tracks, and can include loose and compact rocks. A thick pine forest on the northern side of Jabalcón Mountain also adds varied scenery.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Zújar?
A: Yes, Zújar offers several easy mountain bike routes suitable for families and beginners. While specific routes are not highlighted as 'family-friendly' in the data, the region has 10 easy trails that provide a gentler introduction to mountain biking, allowing families to enjoy the scenic landscapes together.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints along the mountain bike routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views and pass by interesting landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035401759/ojo-de-la-heredad-circular-desde-zujar-por-cerro-jabalcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojo de la Heredad — circular desde Zújar por Cerro Jabalcón&lt;/a&gt; route takes you through Cerro Jabalcón, which offers magnificent viewpoints. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3251692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Narváez Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3128110" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from Horro Mano de Hierro&lt;/a&gt; in the wider area, providing panoramic vistas of the unique landscape.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Zújar?
A: Yes, Zújar has many circular mountain bike routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013417223/morron-alto-desde-baza-circular-por-el-parque-natural-sierra-de-baza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morrón Alto desde Baza — circular por el Parque Natural Sierra de Baza&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 33.5 km trail. Another challenging circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1143313121/vuelta-al-embalse-del-negratin-desde-zujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta al Embalse del Negratín desde Zújar&lt;/a&gt;, which circles the Negratín Reservoir.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Zújar?
A: The mountain bike trails in Zújar are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 120 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the varied terrain, the stunning views, and the options available for all skill levels.

Q: Are there any water features or reservoirs to see along the routes?
A: Absolutely. The expansive Negratín Reservoir is a prominent feature of the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1143313121/vuelta-al-embalse-del-negratin-desde-zujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta al Embalse del Negratín desde Zújar&lt;/a&gt; route specifically circles this large turquoise lake, offering extensive views and opportunities to see water sports. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3251662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain of Health&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Zújar?
A: Zújar, located in the Sierra Nevada region, benefits from a warm climate that makes mountain biking enjoyable year-round. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for longer rides. Summers can be hot, but early mornings or late afternoons are still pleasant. Winters are mild, allowing for continued riding.

Q: Are there any facilities or visitor centers near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4487631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cortijo Narváez Visitor Center&lt;/a&gt; is located in the vicinity and can be a useful stop. One route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cortijo Narváez Visitor Center – Fuente de la Taza loop from Baza&lt;/a&gt;, even starts from there.

Q: What other activities can I combine with mountain biking in Zújar?
A: Beyond mountain biking, Zújar offers a variety of outdoor activities. The Negratín Reservoir is excellent for water sports like windsurfing, kayaking, and swimming. Jabalcón Mountain is popular for paragliding, and the area is also a paradise for hikers and walkers. You can also relax at the natural &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1546278/attractions-around-zujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños de Zújar thermal baths&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Zújar is well-suited for experienced mountain bikers looking for a challenge. There are 41 difficult routes available, characterized by technical descents and varied terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1143313121/vuelta-al-embalse-del-negratin-desde-zujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta al Embalse del Negratín desde Zújar&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 71.4 km path that circles the Negratín Reservoir, offering a significant test of endurance and skill.

Q: What are the elevation gains like on Zújar's mountain bike trails?
A: The elevation gains vary significantly across Zújar's diverse terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1143313121/vuelta-al-embalse-del-negratin-desde-zujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta al Embalse del Negratín desde Zújar&lt;/a&gt; route involves over 1350 meters of ascent, making it quite demanding. Even moderate trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013417223/morron-alto-desde-baza-circular-por-el-parque-natural-sierra-de-baza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morrón Alto desde Baza&lt;/a&gt; can feature over 650 meters of climbing, providing a good workout.
tips: Great views over Embaise de Negratín and the 'Badlands' behind it.
A few meters from the path is the guard house with incredible views of the reservoir. This viewpoint is located at an altitude of 1,100 meters, below Cerro Jabalcón.

Great geological interest is the so-called Piedra Horadada. also known as Horro and Ojo de la Heredad, a natural arch several meters high, formed in the limestone rock of the west face of Jabalcón and next to which this circular track passes, so there will be no difficulty in locating this impressive a place that may well deserve our photographic attention.

waypoints.name: View of Negratín Reservoir from the Guard Hut
